answer: Neuroinflammation / Microglial State Change biomarker panel already links mechanism, translation, and endotype context, so it is the strongest default panel to carry into the paper path.
evidence_chain: Neuroinflammation / Microglial State Change is currently rated supported.
It is tied to 3 Phase 2 transition(s) and 3 downstream object(s).
It already maps to 4 endotype packet(s).
The main open issue is this: The current evidence does not yet show the expected cumulative damage signal.
This item still carries novelty status: Cross Disease Analog.
implication: Neuroinflammation / Microglial State Change biomarker panel will steer future machine runs and slates this way: Future slates use this panel as the default operator readout set.
recommended_follow_up: Select Adopt as the default panel if you want the engine to follow the cleanest current path.
